audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in getentry.c are covered: ldap_first_entry/ldap_next_entry/ldap_get_entry_controls use assert() guards for caller-supplied pointers (standard C API contract); ldap_count_entries uses a proper runtime loop condition. The only unguarded deref is entry->lm_ber at L99, which is safe by library invariant: the sole LDAPMessage allocation site (result.c:997) always assigns lm_ber from a verified non-NULL BerElement, making it impossible to construct a search-entry message with a null lm_ber through any library code path.
